dockerComposeFile.description: The name of the docker-compose file(s) used to start the services.

service.description:
The service you want to work on. This is considered the primary container for your dev
environment which your editor will connect to.

runServices.description: An array of services that should be started and stopped.

workspaceFolder.description:
The path of the workspace folder inside the container. This is typically the target path of
a volume mount in the docker-compose.yml.

shutdownAction.description:
Action to take when the user disconnects from the primary container in their editor. The
default is to stop all of the compose containers.

overrideCommand.description: Whether to overwrite the command specified in the image. The default is false.

$schema.description: The JSON schema of the devcontainer.json file.

name.description: A name for the dev container which can be displayed to the user.

features.description: Features to add to the dev container.

overrideFeatureInstallOrder.description:
Array consisting of the Feature id (without the semantic version) of Features in the order
the user wants them to be installed.

otherPortsAttributes.description:
Set default properties that are applied to all ports that don't get properties from the
setting `remote.portsAttributes`

updateRemoteUserUid.description:
Controls whether on Linux the container's user should be updated with the local user's UID
and GID. On by default when opening from a local folder.

containerUser.description: The user the container will be started with. The default is the user on the Docker image.

init.description: Passes the --init flag when creating the dev container.

privileged.description: Passes the --privileged flag when creating the dev container.

capAdd.description: Passes docker capabilities to include when creating the dev container.

securityOpt.description: Passes docker security options to include when creating the dev container.

remoteEnv.description:
Remote environment variables to set for processes spawned in the
container including lifecycle scripts and any remote editor/IDE server
process.

remoteUser.description:
The username to use for spawning processes in the container including
lifecycle scripts and any remote editor/IDE server process. The default
is the same user as the container.

initializeCommand.description:
A command to run locally (i.e Your host machine, cloud VM) before anything else. This
command is run before "onCreateCommand".

onCreateCommand.description:
A command to run when creating the container. This command is run after "initializeCommand"
and before "updateContentCommand".

updateContentCommand.description:
A command to run when creating the container and rerun when the workspace content was
updated while creating the container. This command is run after "onCreateCommand" and before
"postCreateCommand".

postCreateCommand.description:
A command to run after creating the container. This command is run after
"updateContentCommand" and before "postStartCommand".

postStartCommand.description:
A command to run after starting the container. This command is run after "postCreateCommand"
and before "postAttachCommand".

postAttachCommand.description:
A command to run when attaching to the container. This command is run after
"postStartCommand".

waitFor.description:
The user command to wait for before continuing execution in the background while the UI is
starting up.

customizations.description:
Tool-specific configuration. Each tool should use a JSON object subproperty with a unique
name to group its customizations.

oneOf.description: A port to forward to the primary container.
A `service:port` mapping selecting a compose service.

Template.description: A string that may contain `${...}` variable substitutions. Supported variables: `${localEnv:VAR[:default]}`, `${containerEnv:VAR[:default]}`, `${localWorkspaceFolder}`, `${containerWorkspaceFolder}`, `${localWorkspaceFolderBasename}`, `${containerWorkspaceFolderBasename}`, `${devcontainerId}`. See https://containers.dev/implementors/json_reference/#variables-in-devcontainerjson.

memory.description: Amount of required RAM in bytes. Supports units tb, gb, mb and kb.

storage.description: Amount of required RAM in bytes. Supports units tb, gb, mb and kb.

memory.description: Amount of required RAM in bytes. Supports units tb, gb, mb and kb.

mountGit.description:
Whether to mount the project's git directory into each workspace's devcontainer.

Git worktrees have a simple `.git` file that points to the actual `.git` directory. If that
directory isn't available, then no git commands will work in the worktree. By mounting it
at its original path in the devcontainer, we allow you to use `git` freely for the workspace,
both inside and out of the devcontainer.

Defaults to true, but we use Option so it can be overridden.

proxy.description:
Reverse-proxy configuration.

Leave empty if you don't wish to use it.

ProxyOptions.description:
Per-project proxy configuration. Lives under
`customizations.devconcurrent.proxy` in `devcontainer.json`. The CLI
merges devcontainer.json + project-level overrides and serializes the
result; the proxy reads the same struct back.

Any ports specified in `forwardPorts` are picked up automatically.

enable.description: Opt in to proxy routing for this project.

domainName.description:
Handlebars template for the proxied domain name.

Available variables:
- `root` (bool) — whether this is the root workspace
- `project` — project name
- `workspace` — workspace name
- `service` — name of the service from compose

ProxyHostnameTemplate.description: Handlebars template for the proxied hostname. Variables: `root` (bool), `project`, `workspace`, `service`.

ProxyPort.description:
Port mapping for a single (host, container) pair on a service.

Validation happens at deserialization: TLS termination requires the
`host` port to differ from `container`, since the sidecar needs to bind
`host` for incoming connections while the app binds `container`. A
config-time error is far easier to track down than a runtime sidecar
failure.

ip.description:
The IP address to listen on. Defaults to 0.0.0.0, allowing traffic in
from any source.

tls.description:
Terminate TLS on `host` and forward plaintext to `container`. Requires
`proxy.caRoot` to be configured globally. Default: false.

port.description:
The DNS port the proxy listens on.

Default: 43770

caRoot.description:
Path to mkcert's CAROOT directory on the host. Required for `tls: true`
port mappings; leave unset to disable TLS termination. Find it with
`mkcert -CAROOT`.
